description: Tests whether AI systems can reason about time — validity windows vs decay functions. Covers AsOfQA, ChangeDetection, CausalTrace, StalenessDetection, and Reversion tasks across 4 difficulty versions.

task_families.name: Temporal As-Of Queries
task_families.description: "As of day X, what was true about Y in domain Z?" — core temporal retrieval test.

task_families.name: Change Detection
task_families.description: "Did anything change in domain Z between day X and Y?" — binary change detection.

task_families.name: Causal Trace
task_families.description: "If X had been true at day Y, would Z have been true at day Z?" — counterfactual.

task_families.name: Staleness Detection
task_families.description: "Is this fact stale?" — derived from AsOfQA, measures how often old info is treated as current.

task_families.name: Reversion Detection (Adversarial)
task_families.description: "As of day X, who was CEO of reversion_Y?" — adversarial reversions (flipping facts back).

key_findings: System A paradox: near_accuracy=0%, far_accuracy=73% — standard evaluation misses temporal blindness
D_revised (validity windows) consistently beats D (decay) on hard queries (p<0.001 on v1)
Ablation: removing temporal intervals collapses TRS from 0.68 to 0.31 — intervals are the mechanism
-0.71 temporal_distance correlation in System A: older facts handled better than recent ones
